[Figure: mma-16832-B-i4]
MMA .m16n8k32 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .u4/.s4 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix: B
Operation: MMA m16n8k32
Data type: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit integer) or .s4 (signed 4-bit integer)
Matrix B dimensions: K x N = 32 rows x 8 columns

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has:
- Rows (vertical axis): represent matrix row ranges (0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31), corresponding to K dimension
- Columns (horizontal axis): represent matrix column indices 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (N dimension, 8 columns total)
- Columns 0 and 1 are explicitly shown; column 2 and columns up to 7 are indicated with ".." notation
- Column 7 is explicitly shown

THREAD ASSIGNMENT BY ROW BAND AND COLUMN

Row band 0-7 (K rows 0 through 7):
  Column 0: Thread T0, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T4, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T28, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row band 8-15 (K rows 8 through 15):
  Column 0: Thread T1, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T5, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T29, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row band 16-23 (K rows 16 through 23):
  Column 0: Thread T2, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T6, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T30, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row band 24-31 (K rows 24 through 31):
  Column 0: Thread T3, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T7, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T31, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

THREAD NUMBERING PATTERN
- Threads T0-T3 cover columns 0, rows 0-7 / 8-15 / 16-23 / 24-31 respectively
- Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, same row band pattern
- Each subsequent group of 4 threads covers the next column
- Thread Tx for column c, row band r: T = c*4 + r (where r = 0,1,2,3 for row bands 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31)
- Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
Each thread holds 8 elements labeled b0 through b7 within a single register (shown as a column vector).
Since the data type is 4-bit (.u4 or .s4), 8 elements of 4 bits each = 32 bits total, fitting exactly into one 32-bit register.
So each thread holds exactly 1 register containing 8 packed 4-bit values (b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7).

ELEMENT COVERAGE PER THREAD
Each thread covers 8 consecutive K-rows within its assigned row band and one specific N-column.
The 8 elements b0-b7 correspond to the 8 matrix rows within that thread's row band for its assigned column.

ARROW NOTATION
An arrow is drawn from the cell at row band 0-7 / column 2 area down to row band 24-31 / column 2 area, indicating the ".." columns follow the same pattern as the explicitly shown columns.

SUMMARY TABLE
Row Band  | Col 0 | Col 1 | Col 2 | ... | Col 7
0-7       |  T0   |  T4   |  T8   | ... |  T28
8-15      |  T1   |  T5   |  T9   | ... |  T29
16-23     |  T2   |  T6   |  T10  | ... |  T30
24-31     |  T3   |  T7   |  T11  | ... |  T31

Each thread holds 1 register with 8 packed 4-bit values (b0-b7), totaling 32 bits per register.

    > Figure 89 MMA .m16n8k32 fragment layout for matrix B with `.u4` / `.s4` type.
    >
    > The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =    (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7)      for bi where i = {0,..,7}
  col =     groupID
  ```

  + `.s8` or `.u8` or `.e4m3` or `.e5m2` or `.e3m2` or `.e2m3` or `.e2m1`:

    > | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.s8` / `.u8` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing four `.s8` / `.u8` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7 |
    > | `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing four `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 90](#mma-16832-b-i8-1) and
    > [Figure 91](#mma-16832-b-i8-2).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-16832-B-i8_1]
MMA .m16n8k32 Fragment Layout for Matrix B, Rows 0-15

Data Types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1

Matrix: B (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=32

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows (of the table) represent groups of matrix rows
- Columns (of the table) represent column indices 0, 1, 2, .., 7
- The axis label at the bottom is %laneid:{fragments}

TABLE LAYOUT (Row \ Col):

Row group 0..3, Col 0: T0:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 0..3, Col 1: T4:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 0..3, Col 2: (implied continuation)
Row group 0..3, Col 7: T28: {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row group 4..7, Col 0: T1:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 4..7, Col 1: T5:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 4..7, Col 2: (implied continuation)
Row group 4..7, Col 7: T29: {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row group 8..11, Col 0: T2:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 8..11, Col 1: T6:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 8..11, Col 2: (implied continuation)
Row group 8..11, Col 7: T30: {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row group 12..15, Col 0: T3:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 12..15, Col 1: T7:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 12..15, Col 2: (implied continuation)
Row group 12..15, Col 7: T31: {b0, b1, b2, b3}

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN:
- Threads T0-T3 cover matrix rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively at column group 0
- Threads T4-T7 cover the same row groups at column group 1
- Threads T8-T11 cover the same row groups at column group 2 (implied)
- Threads T28-T31 cover the same row groups at column group 7

General formula:
- Thread index = (col_group * 4) + row_group
  where row_group = 0 for rows 0-3, 1 for rows 4-7, 2 for rows 8-11, 3 for rows 12-15
  and col_group = 0..7

REGISTER PACKING:
Each thread holds 4 elements: b0, b1, b2, b3
These 4 elements are packed into registers as a group (shown as a single brace notation {b0, b1, b2, b3}).
For 8-bit types (u8/s8) or sub-byte types (e4m3/e5m2/e3m2/e2m3/e2m1), multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register. Specifically, 4 elements of 8-bit type fit into one 32-bit register (b0, b1, b2, b3 packed into one register). For sub-byte types, even more elements fit per register.

SUMMARY:
- 32 threads total (T0-T31) cover the full B fragment
- Each thread holds one register containing 4 packed elements (b0-b3)
- Column dimension (N=8, cols 0-7) is distributed across groups of 4 threads (T0,T4,T8,...,T28 for row group 0; T1,T5,T9,...,T29 for row group 1; etc.)
- Row dimension (rows 0-15, grouped into 4 groups of 4 rows) is distributed across threads within each group of 4 (T0-T3 at col 0, T4-T7 at col 1, etc.)
- The arrow in the figure indicates the direction of the %laneid:{fragments} axis spanning columns 0 through 7

MMA .m16n8k32 Fragment Layout for Rows 16-31 of Matrix B
Data Types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows the fragment layout for the SECOND half (rows 16-31) of matrix B in the m16n8k32 MMA instruction. The layout is indexed by Row (groups of 4 matrix rows) and Column (0 through 7, with columns 2 and .. implied between 1 and 7).

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows represent groups of 4 matrix rows (16-19, 20-23, 24-27, 28-31)
- Columns represent 8 column groups (0, 1, 2, .., 7)
- Each cell contains one thread holding 4 elements (b4, b5, b6, b7) packed into registers
- The label "%laneid:{fragments}" indicates thread assignment is by lane ID

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING TABLE:

Row Group 16-19:
  Col 0: T0  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 1: T4  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 2: (implied, pattern continues)
  ...:   (pattern continues)
  Col 7: T28 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row Group 20-23:
  Col 0: T1  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 1: T5  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 2: (implied)
  ...:   (pattern continues)
  Col 7: T29 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row Group 24-27:
  Col 0: T2  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 1: T6  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 2: (implied)
  ...:   (pattern continues)
  Col 7: T30 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row Group 28-31:
  Col 0: T3  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 1: T7  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 2: (implied)
  ...:   (pattern continues)
  Col 7: T31 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN:
- Threads T0-T3 cover row groups 16-19, 20-23, 24-27, 28-31 respectively at column 0
- Threads T4-T7 cover the same row groups at column 1
- Threads T28-T31 cover the same row groups at column 7
- General pattern: Thread T(col*4 + row_group_index) handles the corresponding cell
  where row_group_index = 0 for rows 16-19, 1 for 20-23, 2 for 24-27, 3 for 28-31
  and col ranges 0-7, giving thread numbers 0-31

ELEMENT NAMING AND PACKING:
- Each thread holds elements named b4, b5, b6, b7 (these are the SECOND set of 4 elements, continuing from b0-b3 in rows 0-15)
- For 8-bit types (.u8, .s8, .e4m3, .e5m2, .e3m2, .e2m3, .e2m1): four 8-bit values are packed into registers
- b4 and b5 are packed into one 32-bit register (two 8-bit values per 16-bit half, two halves per register)
- b6 and b7 are packed into a second 32-bit register
- This gives 2 registers per thread for this fragment (b4/b5 in reg0, b6/b7 in reg1), or all four may pack into a single 32-bit register depending on sub-byte packing

ARROW NOTATION:
- A diagonal arrow spans from column 2 row-group 28-31 up to column 7 row-group 16-19, indicating the continuation of the thread numbering pattern across the implied intermediate columns (2 through 6)

SUMMARY:
- Matrix: B, rows 16-31 of K dimension (second half of K=32)
- 32 threads total (T0-T31) each hold elements b4, b5, b6, b7
- Thread assignment: T(4*col + row_group) where col in [0..7], row_group in [0..3]
- Elements b4-b7 are the second quartet of B matrix register fragments per thread

* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing four `.s32` registers, containing four `.s32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
  | `.f32` | A vector expression containing four `.f32` registers, containing four `.f32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
  | `.f16` | A vector expression containing two `.f16x2` registers, with each register containing two `.f16` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |

MMA .m16n8k32 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D

Data Types: .s32 / .f32 / .f16
Matrix: C/D (Accumulator)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=32

GRID STRUCTURE:
The table has rows labeled R\C (Row\Column) representing matrix rows, and columns labeled 0 through 7 representing column pairs of the matrix. The columns are grouped into pairs: (0,1), (2,3), (4,5), (6,7).

##### 9.7.14.5.11. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k64`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16864)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k64` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k64`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s4` / `.u4` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.s4` / `.u4` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a30, a31 |
  | `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.e2m1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a30, a31 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 93](#mma-16864-a).

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 32) + (i & 0x1F)     for ai where i = {0, ...,63}
  ```
